source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 8.1 Partitioning (pp. 126-128)

questions.q: Where is the MBR partition table stored?
options: In the first sector of the disk (the Master Boot Record)
In the last sector of the disk
questions.why: The partition table is in the first sector of the disk (MBR), recording how the physical disk is divided.

questions.q: On an MBR disk, what is the maximum mix of partitions allowed?
options: Up to 4 primary, OR 3 primary plus 1 extended
questions.why: An MBR disk allows at most four partitions: four primary, or three primary plus one extended (only one extended is permitted).

questions.q: How many extended partitions can a single physical disk have (MBR)?
options: None - extended partitions do not exist on MBR
questions.why: There can be only one extended partition per physical disk.

questions.q: What can an extended partition hold?
options: It cannot store data directly or be formatted; it can only contain logical partitions
It stores data directly like a primary partition
It holds the boot loader only
It can hold another extended partition
questions.why: The extended partition is a container: it cannot be formatted or hold data itself, only logical partitions.

questions.q: What is the largest disk an MBR partition table can address?
questions.why: The MBR partition table can recognise disks up to 2 TB; beyond that you need GPT.

questions.q: On an MBR disk, which partition number is the first LOGICAL partition?
questions.why: Numbers 1-4 are reserved for primary/extended partitions, so logical partitions start at 5.
